response.justification: Wei Chen is a skilled software engineer with 15+ years of diverse technical experience, but fails critical mandatory requirements for this role. The position demands 5–10 years of professional C++ development; the resume shows only 6 months (National Instruments, 2014–2015). Additionally, there is no evidence of fixed-income, capital markets, or financial services domain expertise—the only tangential connection is RIDE Capital, with no details on domain or products. The candidate's career trajectory emphasizes web/backend development (Node.js, TypeScript), open-source platforms, and full-stack work rather than systems-level C++ programming or financial systems.
strengths: Strong software engineering fundamentals with 15+ years of professional experience across diverse domains and technologies
Excellent communication and knowledge-sharing abilities, demonstrated through multiple workshops, conference talks, and open-source contributions
Solid CI/CD and DevOps experience, including GitLab infrastructure, deployment pipelines, and containerization
concerns: Only 6 months of professional C++ experience versus the required 5–10 years; primary focus has been web/backend development with TypeScript, Node.js, and D
No demonstrated experience in fixed-income derivatives, capital markets, investment banking, or financial services (except unclear involvement at RIDE Capital with no domain details)
No evidence of Windows platform development, SQL Server or Oracle expertise, or familiarity with capital-markets tools like Summit

key_factors.factor: C++ experience gap (6 months vs. 5–10 years required)
No financial services or capital markets domain knowledge
Strong overall software engineering and communication abilities
